calculated_metrics.confidence_note: This unit cost is estimated by PRISM by dividing total programme expenditure by the total quantified animal beneficiaries.

details.calculation: ($82,059,120 USD / 36,416 total beneficiaries) = $2,253.38 USD per outcome. | A $1,000 USD donation achieves ≈ 0.444 outcomes.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

details.elaboration: ["Quote: In 2024, Animals Asia rolled out a major new bear protection campaign in Vietnam in a renewed push to end bile farming in the country once and for all.", "Highest tractability intervention found: 'Policy And Legal Advocacy'", "EA Rationale: Policy and legal advocacy can create systemic, legally-binding protections for animals, representing a highly tractable path to impact."]
details.formula: Mapping of charity's intervention types against the EA Intervention Tractability evidence base.

details.elaboration: Quote: 'Helped rescue and release over 23,000 songbirds.'
details.formula: Highest level of self-reported evidence cited in impact metrics

details.calculation: No quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Financials with income breakdown are missing.
Focus on high-neglectedness areas (Wild Animals: 63%, Companion Animals: 37%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

metrics.context_qualifier: Over 23,000 songbirds confiscated from illegal traders were rescued and released in Indonesia.
metrics.evidence_quote: Helped rescue and release over 23,000 songbirds.

metrics.context_qualifier: 13,350 shelter dogs and cats in 33 struggling shelters in China received support.
metrics.evidence_quote: Helped 13,350 shelter dogs and cats in 33 struggling shelters in China.

13 moon bears rescued and brought to sanctuaries in Vietnam in 2024.
51 macaques (31, 8, and 12) were rescued, rehabilitated, and released into protected areas in Indonesia.
metrics.evidence_quote: Helped release 31 macaques into protected areas.

metrics.context_qualifier: 10 restaurants stopped selling dog and/or cat meat after being reported to Chinese authorities.
metrics.evidence_quote: Stopped 10 restaurants from selling dog and/or cat meat.

metrics.evidence_quote: Donated 10,400 doses of rabies vaccine to dogs in Vietnamese shelters.

metrics.context_qualifier: 822 physiotherapy sessions provided to rescued bears in 2024.
metrics.evidence_quote: Provided 822 physiotherapy sessions to rescued bears.

metrics.context_qualifier: Over 350,000 kilograms of nutritious food fed to bears at sanctuaries in 2024.
metrics.evidence_quote: Fed the bears at our sanctuaries over 350,000 kilograms of nutritious food, including pumpkin, carrot, sweet potato, cabbage, tomato, apple and pear.

metrics.context_qualifier: Over 150,000 medication pots prepared to help bears take their meds at sanctuaries.
metrics.evidence_quote: Prepared over 150,000 medication pots used to help the bears take their meds.

metrics.context_qualifier: 142,050 marshmallows stuffed with tablets for bears at China sanctuary to aid medication.
metrics.evidence_quote: Stuffed 142,050 marshmallows with tablets for the bears at our China sanctuary.
metrics.metric_name: Marshmallows Stuffed with Tablets for Bears

metrics.context_qualifier: 221 full-body health checks performed on rescued bears, including 23 major surgeries.
metrics.evidence_quote: Performed 221 full-body health checks on our rescued bears, including 23 major surgeries.
metrics.metric_name: Full-Body Health Checks for Rescued Bears

metrics.context_qualifier: 436 companion animals (264 dogs, 172 cats) treated at three smallies clinics in 2024.
metrics.evidence_quote: In 2024, our three smallies clinics treated 436 companion animals - 264 dogs and 172 cats.

metrics.context_qualifier: 161 companion animals (cats and dogs) neutered at clinics in 2024.
metrics.evidence_quote: And of these, 161 were neutered. (All cats and dogs we treat must be de-sexed.)

metrics.context_qualifier: 2 elephants rescued from elephant-riding tourism in Vietnam and brought to sanctuaries.
metrics.evidence_quote: Rescued 2 more elephants from elephant-riding tourism. Kham On and Y'Mam are now free to roam for the rest of their days.

metrics.context_qualifier: 52 campaigns held in China to promote cat and dog welfare.
metrics.evidence_quote: Held 52 campaigns in China to promote cat and dog welfare.
metrics.metric_name: Dog and Cat Welfare Campaigns Held

metrics.evidence_quote: Gave 15 shelter dogs and cats new wheelchairs.
metrics.metric_name: Wheelchairs Provided to Dogs and Cats

metrics.context_qualifier: Food provided for 25 macaques previously helped released.
metrics.evidence_quote: Provided food for 25 macaques we previously helped release.

metrics.evidence_quote: Distributed 3.5 tonnes of rice and dog kibble to shelters in Vietnam.

significant_events.event_name: New Bear Protection Campaign in Vietnam
significant_events.source_quote: In 2024, Animals Asia rolled out a major new bear protection campaign in Vietnam in a renewed push to end bile farming in the country once and for all.
significant_events.summary: Launched a major campaign in Hanoi, a bear bile farming hotspot, to raise community awareness about bear bile farming and herbal alternatives.

significant_events.event_name: Policy Change: Fines for Inhumane Dog/Cat Treatment in Vietnam
significant_events.source_quote: The government is starting to fine people for inhumane treatment of dogs and cats, including beatings and other cruelty at slaughterhouses. The provision was enacted in 2021 but has been enforced until now.
significant_events.summary: Vietnamese government started fining people for inhumane treatment of dogs and cats, including beatings at slaughterhouses. Provision enacted in 2021, enforced in 2024.

significant_events.event_name: Wild Bird Welfare Citizen Science Project
significant_events.source_quote: At the start of 2024, we launched a new citizen science project in China - Wild Bird Welfare - to assess the harm to birds from mist nets.
significant_events.summary: Launched a new citizen science project in China to assess harm to birds from mist nets, involving field surveys and data collection.

significant_events.event_name: Partnership for Stray Cat and Dog Aid in Vietnam
significant_events.source_quote: We've partnered with vegan skin care company Cocoon on a new project, 'Join Hands to Help Stray Cats and Dogs'. In 2024, we: Gave 1.5 tonnes of rice, 2 tonnes of kibble, 4,500 cans of food, treats and medicines to 18 dog and cat shelters.
significant_events.summary: Partnered with vegan skincare company Cocoon on 'Join Hands to Help Stray Cats and Dogs' project in Vietnam, providing food, medicine, and wheelchairs.

significant_events.source_quote: This included building tunnels to connect the macaque enclosures at two Vietnamese facilities - the Hanoi Wildlife Rescue Centre and the Phong Nha Ke Bang Rescue Centre.
significant_events.summary: Built tunnels to connect macaque enclosures at two Vietnamese rescue centers and a second floor for a socialisation enclosure in Indonesia to improve welfare.

significant_events.event_name: Launch of Companion Animal Clinic at Bach Ma
significant_events.source_quote: Our Bach Ma vet team has launched its own Companion Animal Clinic catering to the cats and dogs of employees and families living in and around the sanctuary.
significant_events.summary: Our Bach Ma vet team launched its own Companion Animal Clinic catering to cats and dogs of employees and local families, promoting free vaccinations, flea treatment, deworming and neutering.

significant_events.event_name: Second Bear Sanctuary Phase One Completion
significant_events.source_quote: We completed Phase One of our second bear sanctuary in Vietnam.
significant_events.summary: Completed Phase One of the second bear sanctuary in Vietnam, which will house the country's last surviving bile bears.

significant_events.source_quote: In 2024, we introduced annual physiotherapy assessments for our Tam Dao bears and launched a physio program for our new Bach Ma sanctuary.
significant_events.summary: Introduced annual physiotherapy assessments for Tam Dao bears and launched a physio program for the new Bach Ma sanctuary.

significant_events.event_name: Installation of Concrete Tunnels for Bears
significant_events.source_quote: We installed 10 more at our China sanctuary in 2024, bringing the total number of tunnels there to 25.
significant_events.summary: Installed 10 more giant concrete tunnels at the China sanctuary, bringing the total to 25, providing enrichment for bears.

significant_events.source_quote: Planted 3 new community herbal gardens, bringing the total number across Vietnam to 35. We work with schools and local communities to create the gardens and raise awareness of herbal alternatives to bear bile.
significant_events.summary: Planted 3 new community herbal gardens in Vietnam, bringing the total to 35, to raise awareness of herbal alternatives to bear bile.

significant_events.source_quote: Upgraded a shelter in Quang Binh, building a kitchen, installing partitions to separate dogs and cats, adding a roof and a mesh house to the cat enclosure, and installing a 1,000-litre water tank.
significant_events.summary: Upgraded a shelter in Quang Binh, Vietnam, by building a kitchen, installing partitions, adding a roof, mesh house, and a 1,000-litre water tank.

data.original_text: Animals Asia is dedicated to ending the barbaric practice of bear bile farming and improving animal welfare in China and Vietnam. Over 10,000 bears - mainly moon bears, but also sun bears and brown bears - are held captive on bile farms in China, and around 400 in Vietnam.
We have rescued over 650 bears from this terrible industry enabling them to live without pain or suffering in our rescue centers in China and Vietnam.
In 2021 we undertook the largest Asiatic black bear rescue in the world, which involved moving 101 moon bears from an ex-bile farm in China to our sanctuary in Chengdu.
In 2017, we signed a legally binding agreement with the Vietnamese government that will see us work together to close every farm with the aim of rescuing all remaining bears in Vietnam.
Animals Asia Foundation (AAF) is a Hong Kong–based charity that seeks to end cruelty to animals in Asia.

The organization raises awareness of the inhumane methods used in bear bile farming, which involve bears living up to 25 years in small cages, with metal catheters inserted into their abdomens for bile extraction, or even open wounds through which the bile drips.
The organization also works "to reduce the demand for bile in Asia by promoting affordable, effective and cruelty free alternatives."
Animals Asia has rescued over 670 bears, caring for them at our award-winning bear sanctuaries in China and Vietnam.
We also work against the trade in dogs and cats for food in Asia and lobby to improve the welfare of companion animals and promote humane population management.

We campaign for better conditions in zoos and safari parks in Asia and work closely with governing authorities to improve animal management and increase awareness of the welfare needs of captive wild animals.

We provide training and educational services to animal management professionals, veterinarians, educators, and the public to raise awareness of animal welfare and animal sentience concepts and drive long-term change.
Protect bears, ensure they are free from harm, and provide them with sanctuary.
Improve the welfare of captive wild animals.
Improve the welfare of dogs and cats.
Animals Asia's Cat and Dog welfare team raised donations and delivered over six tons of cat and dog food to support animals impacted by the COVID-19 quarantine in Wuhan.

Animals Asia's Cat and Dog Welfare team achieved a huge victory after receiving news of the new regulation. Stated in China's recent national catalogue of Livestock and Poultry Genetic Resources, it bans the selling of live dogs and dog meat for food.
Animals Asia rescued 10 more bears, bringing to 680 the total number rescued.
We helped to free tens of thousands of trafficked songbirds in Indonesia.
Our therapy dogs visited over 9,000 people in hospitals, schools and care homes.
We completed Phase One of our second bear sanctuary in Vietnam.
We helped rebuild a fire-damaged shelter in China, benefiting 250 cats.
